specialism.name: stale-cleanup
specialism.task: Detect and remove stale editor backup files, orphan temporary source files, and coverage artifacts from the working tree without disturbing any tracked content. These files accumulate across editing sessions (emacs/vim `*~` backups, Julia `*.cov` coverage files, scratch `tmp*.jl` files) and are gitignored but clutter the tree and confuse file searches. The output is a clean working tree with no untracked noise files, verified by `git status` and confirmed with the user before any deletion.

workflow.step:
Enumerate all candidate files by category:
Editor backups: find . -name '*~' -not -path './.git/*'
Coverage artifacts: find . -name '*.cov' -not -path './.git/*'
Orphan tmp source files: find . -name 'tmp*.jl' -not -path './.git/*' find . -name '*.jl~' -not -path './.git/*'
Present results grouped by category before doing anything else.

For each candidate, verify it is untracked (not committed to git): git ls-files --error-unmatch <path> If the command succeeds (exit code 0), the file IS tracked — skip it and warn the user. Only proceed with deletion for files where the command fails (exit code non-zero).

For any candidate with a `.jl` or `.jl~` suffix (source-code orphan, e.g. `tmp.jl`), perform an additional safety check before deletion: grep -rn 'include.*tmp' src/ If the package includes the file via `include(...)`, do NOT delete it — report it to the user as a false positive.

Present a structured deletion proposal to the user grouped by category (see formatting rules) and **wait for explicit confirmation** before executing any `rm` commands. Never perform silent bulk deletion.
After user confirms, delete each confirmed file. Show the resulting `git status` output to verify the working tree is now clean of the listed files.

Optionally, run the test suite to confirm no regression: julia --project test/runtests.jl This step is especially important if any `.jl` or `.jl~` files were removed. Skip if the user confirms the removed files are purely backups with no relation to tested code.

rubric.criterion: No tracked file (one returned by `git ls-files`) is ever deleted. All deletions are limited to files that git reports as untracked or gitignored.
The user explicitly approves the deletion list before any `rm` command executes. Presenting the list and immediately deleting without waiting for a response is a violation.
Any `.jl` or `.jl~` candidate is checked for `include(...)` references in `src/` before deletion is proposed.
After deletion, a summary is provided: how many files were removed per category, and whether any candidates were skipped (tracked, or actively included).
If any source-adjacent files (`.jl~`, `tmp*.jl`) were removed, the test suite is run to confirm nothing broke.

formatting.rule: Present candidates as a table grouped by category before asking for confirmation: | Category | Path | Reason safe to delete | Typical categories: "Editor backup", "Coverage artifact", "Orphan source".
Show the proposed `rm` commands per category so the user can copy-paste to verify or run manually if they prefer. Example: rm docs/src/examples/darcy.md~ docs/src/failure_handling.md~
If any candidate is tracked (git-managed), display it in a separate "SKIP — tracked" section and do NOT include it in the deletion proposal.
After deletion, show `git status` output confirming the stale files are gone.
formatting.example:
## Proposed deletion
Found the following untracked stale files:
### Editor backups (*.md~, *.jl~, *.toml~) | Path | Category | |------|----------| | docs/src/examples/darcy.md~ | Editor backup | | docs/src/failure_handling.md~ | Editor backup | | src/GaussNewtonKalmanInversion.jl~ | Editor backup | | examples/Darcy/Project.toml~ | Editor backup |
Proposed command: ```sh rm docs/src/examples/darcy.md~ docs/src/failure_handling.md~ \ src/GaussNewtonKalmanInversion.jl~ examples/Darcy/Project.toml~ ```
### Coverage artifacts (*.cov) | Path | Category | |------|----------| | test/runtests.jl.416944.cov | Coverage artifact |
Proposed command: ```sh rm test/runtests.jl.416944.cov ```
### Orphan source files (tmp*.jl) | Path | Category | include() check | |------|----------|-----------------| | src/tmp.jl | Orphan source | No include() found — safe |
Proposed command: ```sh rm src/tmp.jl ```
**Awaiting your confirmation before any deletion is executed.**
